methods.description: Process for preparing pollen samples from herbarium specimens and measuring their morphological features using light microscopy.

steps.description: Pollen samples from each of the three studied taxa were obtained from herbarium specimens listed in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain pollen samples from herbarium specimens

steps.description: The identifications of the specimens were made according to the Agropyron accounts given in Flora of Turkey (Melderis, 1985).
steps.searchable_summary: identify specimens using Flora of Turkey

steps.description: For the light microscope study, the pollen grains were prepared following the Wodehouse (1935) and Erdtman (1952) methods.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen grains using Wodehouse and Erdtman methods

steps.description: Morphological features of 30 pollen grains were measured using Leica DM 1000 and their microphotographs were taken by the Leica DFC280 camera attachment.
steps.searchable_summary: measure morphological features and take microphotographs

methods.description: Statistical analysis of pollen measurements to produce phenograms and perform cluster analysis using Gower's General Similarity Coefficient and UPGMA algorithm.

steps.description: Using the average values of pollen measurements, phenograms of the investigated taxa were produced for fresh (W, Wodehouse) and acetolysed (E, Erdtman) pollen grains based on the Gower General Similarity Coefficient.
steps.searchable_summary: produce phenograms using Gower General Similarity Coefficient

steps.description: Gower's (1971) coefficient was chosen to generate a distance matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: generate distance matrix using Gower's coefficient

steps.description: This distance matrix was used for cluster analysis with the help of UPGMA algorithm (Sneath and Sokal, 1973).
steps.searchable_summary: perform cluster analysis using UPGMA algorithm

steps.description: Correlation values were obtained using SPSS version 11.0 (SPSS, 1999).

methods.description: Preparation and examination of pollen grains using scanning electron microscopy to study pollen morphology.

steps.description: For scanning electron microscope (SEM) studies, pollen grains were put on stubs, sputter coated with gold.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen grains for SEM by sputter coating with gold

steps.description: Examined under Jeol JSM-6060LV SEM at the central laboratory of Middle East Technical University.
steps.searchable_summary: examine pollen grains using Jeol JSM-6060LV SEM
